metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `baseline_shadow.py` to the `## Scope (files EDITED)` list so the file manifest is consistent with the Round-5 decision and its AC.
findings.finding: The `## Scope (files EDITED)` section lists exactly six files but omits `baseline_shadow.py`, which the Round-5 resolved decision explicitly adds to the edited-files set and backs with a new AC. An implementer reading only the Scope section will miss the required stdout-print edit to `baseline_shadow.py`.
findings.location: ## Scope (files EDITED) vs. ## Round-5 resolved decision
findings.suggested_fix: Append `- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/baseline_shadow.py` (or whatever the correct path is) to the Scope list, matching the round-5 description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/baseline_shadow.py` to the `## Scope (files EDITED)` section to match the Round-5 resolved decision that explicitly names it as a new edit target.
findings.finding: `baseline_shadow.py` is explicitly added to the edited-files set by the Round-5 resolved decision ('This adds `baseline_shadow.py` to the edited-files set') but is absent from the `## Scope (files EDITED)` section, which still lists exactly six files.
findings.location: ## Scope (files EDITED) / Round-5 resolved decision
findings.suggested_fix: Append `- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/baseline_shadow.py` — add stdout `RECON: baseline_shadow_check divergent=<N> equal=<M>` print to the Scope list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py` to the `## Scope (files EDITED)` section, since it must read `baseline_consumer_swap` from config and thread the bool into `run_differs` (mirroring the `baseline_dual_write` pattern at line 938).
findings.finding: `reconcile.py` is the orchestration layer the plan explicitly identifies as the read-site for the new `baseline_consumer_swap` flag ('Read it ONCE at the orchestration layer (mirror `reconcile.py:936`'s … pattern)'), yet it is not listed in `## Scope (files EDITED)`, making it an unenumerated required edit target.
findings.location: Remaining CODE §1 / ## Scope (files EDITED)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that under `baseline_consumer_swap=True`, the outer guard at `outbound_fields.py:519` (`and prev_jira_fields`) is also replaced by `and baseline` (i.e. the full Site B replacement covers the sentinel guard, not only the `_local_matches_prev` argument at line 523), so conflict detection and direction-suppression use the same ancestor source.
findings.finding: The consumer swap specification at Site B says to pass `baseline` 'wherever `prev_jira_fields` is used by the two `_local_matches_prev` calls', but `prev_jira_fields` is ALSO used as a boolean guard on the outer `if` at line 519 (`if conflict_sink is not None and prev_jira_fields and jira_key:`). If the implementer only replaces the `_local_matches_prev` argument but not this outer guard, the conflict-sink uses the OLD `prev_jira_fields` sentinel while the inner call uses `baseline` — different ancestors for the same check. Under the flag-ON path this could cause: (a) when `prev_jira_fields` is None but `baseline` is not, the outer guard skips conflict detection entirely despite a valid baseline being available; or (b) when `prev_jira_fields` is stale/non-None but `baseline` is None, the outer guard opens but the inner `_local_matches_prev` silently returns False. The plan's phrase 'wherever prev_jira_fields is used by the two _local_matches_prev calls' is ambiguous about whether the outer guard on line 519 is also replaced.
findings.location: Remaining CODE §2 / outbound_fields.py:519 (conflict-sink outer guard)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the Site B specification: 'When the flag is ON, replace BOTH the outer guard (`prev_jira_fields` → `baseline`) AND the `_local_matches_prev` argument at line 523 so the entire conflict-sink block is baseline-consistent. Flag OFF: byte-for-byte the existing `prev_jira_fields` path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Round-5 AC: change `baseline_shadow.py`'s new print to `file=sys.stderr` (matching every existing `RECON:` line) and update the AC text to say 'stderr' — `gh run view --log` captures both streams so the operational streak derivation is unaffected.
findings.finding: The Round-5 AC and its resolution text describe the new `RECON: baseline_shadow_check` print as going to stdout ('matching the existing RECON: stdout convention'), but every existing RECON: diagnostic line in the reconciler goes to stderr (e.g., run_differs.py:480-483 `RECON: outbound_differ`, run_differs.py:586-594 `RECON: inbound_differ`, outbound_differ.py:748-753 `RECON: outbound_update`, outbound_fields.py:512-514 — all `file=sys.stderr`). The codebase convention is stderr, not stdout. pyproject.toml confirms the reconciler's stderr IS its RECON operational output surface. The GHA `gh run view --log` command captures both streams so the operational AC is reachable either way, but the plan's stated convention is factually wrong and will cause implementers to write an inconsistent line that breaks the stderr-only RECON diagnostic pattern.
findings.location: ## Round-5 resolved decision / ## Acceptance Criteria (round-5 addition) — `baseline_shadow.py` stdout print AC
findings.suggested_fix: In baseline_shadow.py, emit: `print(f'RECON: baseline_shadow_check divergent={divergent} equal={equal}', file=sys.stderr, flush=True)` — remove 'stdout' from the Round-5 AC description and replace with 'stderr'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Round-5 AC to print `RECON: baseline_shadow_check divergent=<N> equal=<M>` to **stderr** (matching the actual RECON: convention used in all existing reconciler prints) and update the unit-test assertion accordingly; confirm that `gh run view --log` still finds the line (it captures both streams).
findings.finding: The Round-5 resolution asserts that the new stdout print 'matches the existing RECON: stdout convention', but every existing RECON: print in the codebase uses `file=sys.stderr` — there is no stdout RECON convention. This is a verifiable capability assertion that is incorrect: `run_differs.py:243-246` even documents that stderr is chosen specifically to keep stdout a 'pure JSON payload for library/MCP callers'. Printing the baseline_shadow_check line to stdout instead of stderr could interfere with library-mode consumers.
findings.location: Round-5 resolved decision — baseline_shadow.py stdout print; AC '[CODE] baseline_shadow.py prints a RECON: baseline_shadow_check... line to stdout (flush=True)'
findings.suggested_fix: In `baseline_shadow.py`, use `print(f'RECON: baseline_shadow_check divergent={divergent} equal={equal}', file=sys.stderr, flush=True)` — matching run_differs.py:480-483 exactly. Update the AC and unit test to capture stderr instead of stdout.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Round-5 AC and resolved-decision text: the new `RECON: baseline_shadow_check` line must go to **stderr** (`file=sys.stderr`, matching every existing `RECON:` peer) — update both the prose ('stdout convention' → 'stderr convention') and the `baseline_shadow.py` implementation spec accordingly; the `gh run view --log | grep` operational AC remains achievable since GHA captures stderr.
findings.finding: The Round-5 resolved decision states the new `RECON: baseline_shadow_check` line goes to **stdout** ('matching the existing `RECON:` stdout convention'), but every existing `RECON:` diagnostic line in the reconciler goes to **stderr** (`file=sys.stderr`): `run_differs.py:483`, `run_differs.py:590,594`, `outbound_differ.py:748-753`, `outbound_fields.py:433-514`, `applier.py:796-799`. There is no stdout `RECON:` convention; the convention is stderr. `gh run view --log` captures both streams in GHA, so the operational grep works either way — but an implementer following 'stdout (flush=True)' will produce a line inconsistent with all peer `RECON:` emitters, silently violating the de facto stderr contract and breaking any tooling that reads only stderr for RECON diagnostics.
findings.location: ## Round-5 resolved decision — 'prints a `RECON: baseline_shadow_check divergent=<N> equal=<M>` line to stdout (flush=True), matching the existing `RECON:` stdout convention'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Round-5 resolution from `print('RECON: baseline_shadow_check ...', flush=True)` (implied stdout) to `print('RECON: baseline_shadow_check ...', file=sys.stderr, flush=True)` and update the rationale from 'stdout convention' to 'stderr convention'. The unit-test AC should capture sys.stderr (not sys.stdout) to assert the line.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Round-5 resolution to specify `file=sys.stderr` (matching the verified `RECON:` convention used by every existing reconciler diagnostic print), and confirm that `gh run view --log` captures stderr — removing the contradictory 'stdout convention' claim.
findings.finding: The Round-5 resolution specifies printing `RECON: baseline_shadow_check divergent=<N> equal=<M>` to **stdout** and explicitly claims this 'matches the existing RECON: stdout convention' — but code-verified reality is that every `RECON:` diagnostic print in the reconciler uses `file=sys.stderr` (outbound_fields.py:436,446,474,514; outbound_differ.py:753; applier.py:799; run_differs.py:483,590,594; etc.). There is no stdout `RECON:` convention; the described convention does not exist. An implementor following the plan literally will write to stdout while every sibling `RECON:` line goes to stderr — an inconsistency that also affects whether `gh run view --log` (which merges both streams in GHA) actually sees the line reliably.
findings.location: ## Round-5 resolved decision / Round-5 AC: `baseline_shadow.py` prints `RECON: baseline_shadow_check` to stdout (flush=True)
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Round-5 AC to: '`baseline_shadow.py` prints a `RECON: baseline_shadow_check divergent=<N> equal=<M>` line to **stderr** (`file=sys.stderr, flush=True`), matching the existing `RECON:` diagnostic convention used throughout the reconciler (outbound_fields.py, applier.py, run_differs.py, etc.).' Note: GHA captures both stdout and stderr in `gh run view --log`, so the operational grep AC is unaffected.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a post-flip live-observation AC: after `baseline_consumer_swap=true` is deployed, require at least one `--mode live` (or `--mode dry-run` with flag ON) reconcile pass to complete, capture its stdout, and assert the `RECON: baseline_shadow_check` line and reconciler exit are as expected — record the GHA run-id as evidence before closing.
findings.finding: After the flag is actually flipped ON (`baseline_consumer_swap = true`), no acceptance criterion requires running even one live reconcile pass with the flag ON to observe that the new `get_baseline`-based code path behaves correctly in production. The shadow passes (gate 1) run with flag=false; the unit/property tests run offline; the rollback rehearsal reverts to flag=false before the dry-run. The new code path (`get_baseline` replacing `prev_snapshot`) is never exercised end-to-end in a live production-equivalent run by any AC.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Flip the flag only after BOTH gates' bullet; AC '[OPERATIONAL] Flip only after both gates; rollback rehearsed (flag-revert + dry-run run-ids recorded).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an OPERATIONAL AC: '[OPERATIONAL] Post-flip observation: after flipping baseline_consumer_swap=true, run one reconcile-bridge pass (--mode dry-run or live) with the flag ON; grep the run's stdout for `RECON: baseline_shadow_check divergent=0` and confirm no unexpected mutations; record the GHA run-id on this ticket. Only after this pass is the flip considered complete.' This satisfies E6(f) with a live execution of the new code path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the expected post-rollback schema state of both `bindings.json` and live Jira for a mid-heal partial failure: either (a) assert the heal is atomic (all deletions succeed or none are applied, e.g. via a dry-run pre-check list with a single-batch cap), or (b) document that mid-heal rollback requires compensating-forward (manually retire the partially-deleted bindings in `bindings.json` to match Jira state) rather than a clean git-tag restore.
findings.finding: The live heal's post-partial-failure rollback state is under-specified: if Jira-side GC-after-grace deletions complete for some issues and then the heal fails, restoring bindings.json from the git tag produces a store that still marks those bindings as 'confirmed' (pre-heal state), causing the next reconciler pass to attempt to re-process already-deleted Jira issues.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal' section; AC: 'Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either name all consumers of `binding_walk_census` and confirm none are external (making the single-step drop safe), OR split item 4 into two commits: first emit BOTH keys, then drop `binding_walk_census` after confirming zero consumers remain — and update AC4 to verify the sequencing.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract (CODE item 4 / AC4) drops `binding_walk_census` in the same step that introduces `decision_census`, with no sequencing window for any downstream consumer to migrate. The plan says 'migrate any consumer' but neither names the consumers nor sequences a two-phase emit-both-then-drop, so if any consumer (dashboard, alert, external script) reads `binding_walk_census`, the drop is an unacknowledged breaking change.
findings.location: ## Remaining CODE item 4; Acceptance Criteria AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit pre-heal step to disable or pause the `reconcile-bridge.yml` scheduled workflow (e.g., `gh workflow disable reconcile-bridge.yml`) before executing the live heal, and re-enable it after heal + verification complete; document this as a required operational step mirroring the A3 coordination pattern.
findings.finding: The reconcile-bridge workflow fires every ~20 minutes with no explicit guard preventing a scheduled reconciler run from overlapping with the agent-driven live heal. The plan mentions that A3's live remediation 'pauses the reconciler and RESETS this streak' but does not instruct the agent to pause/disable the reconcile-bridge workflow before executing the live heal in this plan — creating a window where a scheduled reconciler pass and the manual heal mutate the same Jira issues concurrently.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal' bullet; 'COORDINATION: A3's live remediation pauses the reconciler' note

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that `local_id` and `baseline_consumer_swap` will be added to `_diff_fields` as keyword-only parameters with defaults (`local_id: str = ""`, `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False`), or enumerate the ~5 test files whose `_diff_fields` call-sites must be updated.
findings.finding: Adding `local_id` and a `baseline_consumer_swap` flag to `_diff_fields`'s signature will break ~20 existing direct call-sites across at least five test files (`test_outbound_differ_adf_extraction.py`, `test_inbound_field_sync_directionality.py`, `test_diff_assignee_unmappable_converges.py`, `test_diff_assignee_permissive.py`, `test_outbound_field_conflict_and_drop_alerts.py`) that call `differ._diff_fields(...)` with the current positional/keyword signature. The plan neither enumerates these as callers requiring updates nor specifies that the new parameters will be keyword-only with defaults (which would make them backward-compatible).
findings.location: Remaining CODE §2 / outbound_fields.py `_diff_fields` signature
findings.suggested_fix: Add both new parameters as keyword-only with safe defaults so all existing callers remain valid without changes: `def _diff_fields(ticket, jira_fields, *, …, local_id: str = "", baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False) -> dict`.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the updated `_diff_fields` signature to item 2's plumbing description: specify `local_id: str = ''` and `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False` as new keyword-only parameters (after a bare `*` or appended as keyword-only after existing kwargs), so the flag-OFF path is byte-for-byte unchanged and no existing call site breaks.
findings.finding: The swap plumbing section (Remaining CODE item 2) says to 'thread `local_id` (already in scope at `outbound_differ.py:622`) into the `_diff_fields` call ... plus the `baseline_consumer_swap` bool' — but `_diff_fields` currently has no `local_id` parameter in its signature (`outbound_fields.py:333-343`). The plan specifies the caller-side threading but not the updated callee signature: specifically whether `local_id` is a new keyword-only argument, a positional argument, where it sits relative to existing parameters (`binding_store`, `prev_jira_fields`, etc.), and what its default value is (required for the flag-OFF backward-compatible path and for any call sites not in `_compute_outbound_update_mutation`).
findings.location: ## Remaining CODE item 2 / Plumbing paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Add to item 2: '`_diff_fields` gains two new keyword-only parameters: `local_id: str = ""` and `baseline_consumer_swap: bool = False`. Default `""` for `local_id` ensures the flag-OFF path is byte-for-byte identical to today (an empty local_id with flag=False never calls `get_baseline`). All existing call sites other than `_compute_outbound_update_mutation` are unaffected.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the capped live heal is idempotent (or define a re-entry safe procedure): either each heal write is a no-op if already applied (idempotent Jira update), or the agent records a per-issue heal-status checkpoint that survives a restart so a re-run skips already-healed issues and does not double-mutate.
findings.finding: The capped live heal writes to live Jira across potentially multiple issues (REB-530 GC-after-grace deletion), but the plan specifies no idempotency guarantee or partial-failure recovery path for the heal itself. If the Jira API fails mid-heal after some writes succeed, the bilateral backup enables manual rollback but the plan does not specify whether the heal operation is re-runnable (idempotent) or leaves the system in a partially-healed state that is safe without manual intervention.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal' bullet; AC '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'
findings.suggested_fix: Before each per-issue heal write, check current Jira state and skip if already matches the target (idempotent PUT semantics). Alternatively, write a per-issue `healed: true` marker to a local checkpoint file (outside `bridge_state/`) before moving to the next issue, so a resumed run can skip completed items.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before dropping `binding_walk_census`, add an explicit step to audit all downstream consumers (monitoring dashboards, alerting rules, runbooks, grep patterns) that reference the key by name, and update or deprecate them — document the audit outcome alongside the one-line rollback note.
findings.finding: The plan drops `binding_walk_census` from the emitted census record without calling out that any downstream operational consumers (monitoring dashboards, alerting rules, log-grep runbooks) referencing that key by name need to be identified and updated before the drop lands. The only mitigation documented is a code-level one-line rollback; silent breakage of external consumers is not addressed.
findings.location: Remaining CODE §4 (census expand-contract); AC: 'binding_walk_census dropped WITH a documented one-line rollback'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-step to the census expand-contract work item: 'grep -r binding_walk_census .github/ docs/ monitoring/ alerts/ (or equivalent) to enumerate all non-code consumers; update each before the drop commit lands.' Record the audit result in the commit message or a follow-up note on this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before dropping `binding_walk_census`, enumerate all consumers (grep codebase, dashboards, alert configs, runbooks) that reference the key; either confirm there are none external to `run_differs.py` or coordinate their migration before the drop; record the enumeration result in the commit message or a doc comment.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract drops `binding_walk_census` after migrating 'any consumer', but the plan does not enumerate what downstream consumers exist (dashboards, alert rules, log-based monitors, external scripts) that read the `binding_walk_census` key from logs or metrics. Dropping the key without a prior enumeration risks silently breaking untracked consumers.
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 4 — Census expand-contract; AC '[CODE] Census emits decision_census; consumers migrated; binding_walk_census dropped WITH a documented one-line rollback'
findings.suggested_fix: Run `grep -r 'binding_walk_census' .` across the repo and any linked dashboard/alert config repos; if the only occurrence is `run_differs.py:652`, document that explicitly in the PR. If external consumers exist, migrate them first (or add a parallel emit period) before dropping the key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit dual-emit window to the census expand-contract: emit BOTH `decision_census` AND `binding_walk_census` in the same record for at least one deploy cycle, confirm downstream consumers are reading `decision_census`, then drop `binding_walk_census` in a follow-on commit; assert the post-rollback schema state (both keys present vs only `binding_walk_census`) explicitly in the rollback procedure.
findings.finding: The census rename (binding_walk_census → decision_census) does not provide a simultaneous dual-emit overlap window, and the plan does not assert the schema state after rollback.
findings.location: Remaining CODE §4 (Census expand-contract); AC: 'Census emits decision_census; consumers migrated; binding_walk_census dropped WITH a documented one-line rollback'
findings.suggested_fix: Split the census task into two commits: (1) add `decision_census` emit alongside `binding_walk_census`; (2) after confirming consumers, drop `binding_walk_census`. The rollback of commit (2) returns to both keys present; the rollback of commit (1) returns to only `binding_walk_census`. Document the target state after each rollback.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify unambiguously in item 4 whether `decision_census` is produced by (a) renaming the `binding_walk_census` key while keeping `walk.census` as the dict, or (b) calling `classify.census(walk.decisions, ...)` at the emit site — and confirm the emitted dict shape matches what `classify.census()` documents.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract (Remaining CODE item 4) says to 'Emit `decision_census` ... from the same `census(...)` record currently shipped under `binding_walk_census`' — but `walk.census` (the dict logged at `run_differs.py:696`) is a pre-computed attribute on the `BindingWalkResult`; it is NOT produced by calling `classify.census()` at the emit site. The plan does not specify whether the implementation should (a) simply rename the `sync_logger.log` key from `binding_walk_census` to `decision_census` reusing `walk.census`, (b) call `classify.census()` on `walk`'s decisions to produce a freshly-computed dict, or (c) something else. The processing rule — which code path produces the dict that gets logged under `decision_census` — is unspecified, leaving the implementor to guess.
findings.location: ## Remaining CODE item 4 / AC: 'Census emits `decision_census`; consumers migrated; `binding_walk_census` dropped'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the `RECON:` stdout convention (prefix format, flush=True requirement, list of currently emitted `RECON:` lines) in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` or `CLAUDE.md` so future contributors have an authoritative reference.
findings.finding: The Round-5 addition introduces a new observable `RECON: baseline_shadow_check divergent=<N> equal=<M>` stdout line as a durable operational signal, but no convention document (CLAUDE.md, runbook, or docs/ page) is updated to record the `RECON:` prefix convention, its format contract, or the list of known `RECON:` lines. Future contributors adding new stdout signals have no authoritative reference to follow.
findings.location: Round-5 resolved decision; Round-5 AC: 'baseline_shadow.py prints a RECON: baseline_shadow_check...'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short '## Stdout RECON signals' section to docs/jira-sync-setup.md listing each known `RECON: <key> <k=v...>` line, its source file, and its purpose; cross-link it from the streak-derivation AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [G5 advisory] Consider whether the census expand-contract (emit `decision_census`, drop `binding_walk_census`) should be extracted to its own commit/child so a census-rename incident doesn't force reverting the consumer swap — the two changes have independent rollback paths and no causal coupling.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract (emit `decision_census`, migrate consumers, drop `binding_walk_census`) has a distinct reason to change — it serves a different downstream consumer/persona (observability/monitoring consumers of census keys) — and is independently releasable without coupling to the consumer swap or the operational flip gate. Its rollback is even documented separately ('re-add the `binding_walk_census` emit — one line'). This meets the single-concern decomposition signal: a structural 'and' joining the swap rollout and an unrelated census rename.
findings.location: Remaining CODE item 4 + AC '[CODE] Census emits `decision_census`...'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'bootstrap-strict' inline (e.g., 'bootstrap-strict mode: `rebar heal --mode bootstrap-strict`, which enforces X') and state the explicit cap value for the live heal (e.g., 'capped at N issues, enforced by the existing `check_blast_radius=0.10` guard') so the executor can proceed without clarification.
findings.finding: 'Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict' references two undefined terms: (1) 'bootstrap-strict' is a named mode never defined in this plan, and (2) the cap value for the 'capped live heal' is never stated — an executor must infer from context (possibly `check_blast_radius=0.10`) or guess.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict incl. the GC-after-grace deletion (f8b5-2f30 → REB-530)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical after 'bootstrap-strict': e.g., '(i.e., `--mode bootstrap-strict` — heals only issues whose baseline is absent, skipping any active binding already arbitrated)' and state 'cap = check_blast_radius limit of 0.10 (max 10% of active bindings mutated per pass, inherited from the already-landed circuit breaker)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [E3] Enumerate all known consumers of `binding_walk_census` (monitoring dashboards, alerting rules, log parsers, downstream pipeline code) and confirm the scope of 'migrate any consumer' — if only `run_differs.py` is the sole emitter and no external consumer exists, state that explicitly; if external consumers exist, add them to the edited-files scope or document a migration window.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract task says 'migrate any consumer' of `binding_walk_census` to `decision_census` before dropping it, but the plan does not enumerate what those consumers are beyond the single emit in `run_differs.py:652`. External consumers — monitoring dashboards, log parsers, alerting rules, or downstream pipeline code — that depend on the `binding_walk_census` key would break silently, and the plan does not acknowledge them as callers depending on the old behavior.
findings.location: Remaining CODE §4 (census expand-contract) and Acceptance Criteria: 'Census emits `decision_census`; consumers migrated; `binding_walk_census` dropped'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence audit result to the census AC: e.g. 'Audit confirms `binding_walk_census` is emitted only at `run_differs.py:652` and consumed only by [list or "no external consumers"]; external monitoring uses `decision_census` from [date].' If external consumers exist, add a deprecation period before the drop.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `_BASELINE_FIELDS` line citation from `binding_store.py:46-54` to `binding_store.py:71-77` in the 'Key facts established' section.
findings.finding: `binding_store.py:46-54` is cited to ground the shape of `_BASELINE_FIELDS` (the namespace-compatibility claim), but those lines are the docstring of `BindingPersistError`, not `_BASELINE_FIELDS`. The tuple is actually defined at lines 71-77.
findings.location: Key facts established — '`_BASELINE_FIELDS = ("summary","description","priority","status","assignee")`, `binding_store.py:46-54`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `binding_store.py:46-54` with `binding_store.py:71-77` in the plan's namespace-compatibility fact bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `get_baseline` line citation from `binding_store.py:314-318` to `binding_store.py:346-360` in the 'Key facts established' section.
findings.finding: `binding_store.py:314-318` is cited to ground the `get_baseline` None-return contract ('returns `None` for BOTH absent and non-dict baseline'), but lines 314-318 are `bind_create_pending`'s body, not `get_baseline`. The method is at lines 346-360.
